semantic.role: Documentation and governance mirror system for software architecture
responsibilities: Indexing code structure and symbols across multiple languages
Validating code against declarative architectural rules
Generating visual views (architecture diagrams, risk hotspots, project health)
Providing CLI commands and MCP server for interactive governance
Managing multi-language AST extraction and dependency graphs
Integrating with CI/CD pipelines and pre-commit hooks
Supporting multilingual documentation via atlas (English, Simplified Chinese)
outOfScope: Direct code editing or automated refactoring beyond suggested fixes
Runtime monitoring, logging, or performance analysis

English.purpose: ArchSpine exists to create a living ‘mirror’ of a software system’s architecture — a structured index that enforces rules, detects drift, and surfaces actionable insights for both humans and AI agents. It turns architectural intent into a machine-readable, continuously validated spine.
English.context_and_audience: This document describes ArchSpine for software architects, lead developers, and DevOps engineers who need to maintain architectural integrity across a polyglot codebase. It is also aimed at teams who want to automate governance, generate up-to-date architectural documentation, and provide reliable context for AI-assisted development workflows.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine uses a .spine directory as a single source of truth for architecture metadata, rules, and indexes.
It supports CLI, MCP, and CI integrations, making it accessible in daily development and automated pipelines.
The system detects violations of layered architectures, naming conventions, and custom rules, with optional LLM-powered auto-fix.
Generated views include architecture diagrams, risk hotspots, public surface maps, and project health dashboards.
Documentation is stored in a multilingual atlas (English and Simplified Chinese) and synchronized with the codebase.
